reasoning: Bobby Finke set the 1500m freestyle long course world record at the Paris Olympics in August 2024 (14:30.67). Prior to this, the record had stood for 12 years (since 2012). The upcoming resolution dates are in November 2025, December 2025, and February 2026. This period corresponds to the traditional short course season and off-season for major international long course competitions. Because long course world records in distance events require peak form usually reserved for the Olympics or World Championships, the likelihood of anyone breaking the record during this winter window is extremely low. The only other significant risk is a retroactive doping disqualification or administrative error on the Wikipedia page, both of which are also highly unlikely given Finke's profile and lack of any associated controversies. I'm providing high probabilities for all dates, slightly decreasing for the later ones to reflect the growing, albeit very small, uncertainty of unexpected events over time.
